posts.name: Alessandro Tarabini
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I would like to have your feedback on a behaviour that I see in my toys that I do not fully understand. I am working with a (simplified) datacard to measure cross sections in the H(GammaGamma) channel in bins of transverse momentum of the Higgs. Datacard and workspace (after compilation with t2w) can be found here: /afs/cern.ch/work/a/atarabin/public/powheg_dc
I am generating and fitting toys with the following commands:
combine Datacard_PTH_2023.root
-M GenerateOnly
-m 125
--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy=0
--saveWorkspace
--X-rtd MINIMIZER_skipDiscreteIterations
--setParameters MH=125,r_PTH_0p0_15p0=1,r_PTH_15p0_30p0=1,r_PTH_30p0_45p0=1,r_PTH_45p0_80p0=1,r_PTH_80p0_120p0=1,r_PTH_120p0_200p0=1,r_PTH_200p0_350p0=1,r_PTH_350p0_10000p0=1
-t 1
--saveToys
--seed $seed

combine Datacard_PTH_2023.root
--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy=0
--saveWorkspace
--X-rtd MINIMIZER_skipDiscreteIterations
--setParameters MH=125,r_PTH_0p0_15p0=1,r_PTH_15p0_30p0=1,r_PTH_30p0_45p0=1,r_PTH_45p0_80p0=1,r_PTH_80p0_120p0=1,r_PTH_120p0_200p0=1,r_PTH_200p0_350p0=1,r_PTH_350p0_10000p0=1
--setParameterRanges r_PTH_350p0_10000p0=-30,30
--saveFitResult
--floatOtherPOIs 1
--freezeParameters MH,${pdfindex},allConstrainedNuisances
-D higgsCombineTest.GenerateOnly.mH125.${seed}.root:toys/toy_1
-M MultiDimFit
-m 125

I am removing the discrete profiling and freezing systematic uncertainties to remove possible sources of bias. Of the total toys I generated, I have 23,253 succeeding fits (fraction of failing fits is 13.36%).
The distributions of my POIs is dumped in the slides in attachment. For 'r_PTH_15p0_30p0, r_PTH_30p0_45p0, r_PTH_45p0_80p0, r_PTH_120p0_200p0, and r_PTH_200p0_350p0, the distributions look good: a distribution around 1.
I am a bit perplexed by the first r_PTH_0p0_15p0 and last r_PTH_350p0_10000p0 bin.

r_PTH_0p0_15p0 The distribution is shifted below of ~7%. Any wise suggestion on how to identify the origin of the bias?
r_PTH_200p0_350p0 The distribution is very skewed and with a long left tail (it is cut at -30 because the parameter range is up to -30). I believe this is somewhat expected since this is the last PTH bin, where the statistics is very low. Looking at the entire distribution, the median, which should be a more robust estimator that the mean in these cases, is ~ 1. If we zoom in, it is a bit trickier, it looks that the actual peak is more around ~1.4. I would expect in any case a distribution peaking at one, no matter the statistics. I am a bit perplexed whether this bin is behaving healthy or not.

When running the Asimov fit, all the bin close perfectly to one.
Any inputs/opinions on these results will be very much appreciated.
Cheers,
Alessandro
toys.pdf (557.1 KB) (The grey line is at 1 as a reference)
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I would like to have your feedback on a behaviour that I see in my toys that I do not fully understand. I am working with a (simplified) datacard to measure cross sections in the H(GammaGamma) channel in bins of transverse momentum of the Higgs. Datacard and workspace (after compilation with t2w) can be found here: /afs/cern.ch/work/a/atarabin/public/powheg_dc

I am generating and fitting toys with the following commands:

combine Datacard_PTH_2023.root 
        -M GenerateOnly 
        -m 125 
        --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy=0 
        --saveWorkspace 
        --X-rtd MINIMIZER_skipDiscreteIterations 
        --setParameters MH=125,r_PTH_0p0_15p0=1,r_PTH_15p0_30p0=1,r_PTH_30p0_45p0=1,r_PTH_45p0_80p0=1,r_PTH_80p0_120p0=1,r_PTH_120p0_200p0=1,r_PTH_200p0_350p0=1,r_PTH_350p0_10000p0=1 
        -t 1 
        --saveToys 
        --seed $seed

combine Datacard_PTH_2023.root 
        --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy=0 
        --saveWorkspace 
        --X-rtd MINIMIZER_skipDiscreteIterations 
        --setParameters MH=125,r_PTH_0p0_15p0=1,r_PTH_15p0_30p0=1,r_PTH_30p0_45p0=1,r_PTH_45p0_80p0=1,r_PTH_80p0_120p0=1,r_PTH_120p0_200p0=1,r_PTH_200p0_350p0=1,r_PTH_350p0_10000p0=1 
        --setParameterRanges r_PTH_350p0_10000p0=-30,30 
        --saveFitResult 
        --floatOtherPOIs 1 
        --freezeParameters MH,${pdfindex},allConstrainedNuisances 
        -D higgsCombineTest.GenerateOnly.mH125.${seed}.root:toys/toy_1 
        -M MultiDimFit 
        -m 125

I am removing the discrete profiling and freezing systematic uncertainties to remove possible sources of bias. Of the total toys I generated, I have 23,253 succeeding fits (fraction of failing fits is 13.36%).

The distributions of my POIs is dumped in the slides in attachment. For 'r_PTH_15p0_30p0, r_PTH_30p0_45p0, r_PTH_45p0_80p0, r_PTH_120p0_200p0, and r_PTH_200p0_350p0, the distributions look good: a distribution around 1.

I am a bit perplexed by the first r_PTH_0p0_15p0 and last r_PTH_350p0_10000p0 bin.

  • r_PTH_0p0_15p0 The distribution is shifted below of ~7%. Any wise suggestion on how to identify the origin of the bias?
  • r_PTH_200p0_350p0 The distribution is very skewed and with a long left tail (it is cut at -30 because the parameter range is up to -30). I believe this is somewhat expected since this is the last PTH bin, where the statistics is very low. Looking at the entire distribution, the median, which should be a more robust estimator that the mean in these cases, is ~ 1. If we zoom in, it is a bit trickier, it looks that the actual peak is more around ~1.4. I would expect in any case a distribution peaking at one, no matter the statistics. I am a bit perplexed whether this bin is behaving healthy or not.

When running the Asimov fit, all the bin close perfectly to one.

Any inputs/opinions on these results will be very much appreciated.

Cheers,
Alessandro

toys.pdf (557.1 KB) (The grey line is at 1 as a reference)

posts.name: Alessandro Tarabini
posts.text: PS. Typo, the second bullet refers to r_PTH_350p0_10000p0
posts.cooked_html: PS. Typo, the second bullet refers to r_PTH_350p0_10000p0

posts.name: Aliya Nigamova
posts.text:
Dear Alessandro,
I would try investigate what happens with other parameters in your model in these toys.
To start with you can try to identify a set of the most (anti)-correlated parameters, save them in the toys with `–saveSpecifiedNuis=par1,par2,…` (see docs) and plot in a similar way, and see about the next steps from what you find.
Hope it helps,
Aliya
posts.cooked_html:

I would try investigate what happens with other parameters in your model in these toys.
To start with you can try to identify a set of the most (anti)-correlated parameters, save them in the toys with `–saveSpecifiedNuis=par1,par2,…` (see docs) and plot in a similar way, and see about the next steps from what you find.

Hope it helps,
Aliya
